sections.title: Section A
sections.header_notes: Attempt all questions. Each question carries 2 marks.

subquestions.text: Form the partial differential equation by eliminating the arbitrary constants 'a' and 'b' from the relation z = (x + a)(y + b).
Determine the nature of the second-order partial differential equation: (x^2)u_{xx} + 2xyu_{xy} + (y^2)u_{yy} = 0.
subquestions.topic: Classification of linear partial differential equation of second order

subquestions.text: Calculate the first moment about the origin for the set of numbers {2, 4, 6, 8, 10}.
If a random variable X follows a Poisson distribution such that P(X=1) = P(X=2), find the value of P(X=0).
A continuous random variable X has a probability density function f(x) = kx for 0 < x < 2 and 0 otherwise. Determine the value of the constant k.
subquestions.topic: Probability mass function and Probability density function

subquestions.text: Find the standard error of the mean for a sample of size 400 drawn from an infinite population with a standard deviation of 15.
Calculate the degrees of freedom for a Chi-square test applied to a 4x3 contingency table.

sections.title: Section B
sections.header_notes: Attempt all questions. Each question carries 7 marks.

subquestions.text: Solve the non-linear partial differential equation p^2 + q^2 = x + y using Charpit's method.
Solve the one-dimensional heat equation du/dt = c^2 (d^2u/dx^2) given the boundary conditions u(0, t) = 0, u(L, t) = 0 and the initial condition u(x, 0) = x(L-x).
subquestions.topic: Solution of wave and heat conduction equation up to two dimension

subquestions.text: Fit a second-degree parabola of the form y = a + bx + cx^2 to the following data using the method of least squares: (x: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4) and (y: 1, 1.8, 1.3, 2.5, 6.3).

In a certain factory, machines A, B, and C produce 20%, 30%, and 50% of the total output respectively. Of their output, 5%, 4%, and 2% are defective. If an item is drawn at random and is found to be defective, compute the probability that it was produced by machine B.

A random sample of 10 individuals has the following IQs: 70, 120, 110, 101, 88, 83, 95, 98, 107, 100. Test the hypothesis that the population mean IQ is 100 at 5% level of significance. (Given t-table value for 9 d.f. at 5% is 2.262)

sections.title: Section C
sections.header_notes: Attempt one part from each question. Each part carries 7 marks.
sections.attempt_rule: Attempt any one part of each question

options.label: a
options.text: Solve the linear partial differential equation: (x^2 - y^2 - z^2)p + 2xyq = 2xz using Lagrange's method.

options.label: b
options.text: Find the complete solution of the higher-order PDE: (D^2 - 4DD' + 4D'^2)z = e^(2x+y).
options.topic: Solution of Linear Partial Differential Equation of Higher order with constant coefficients

options.label: a
options.text: A tightly stretched string of length L with fixed ends is initially at rest in its equilibrium position. If it is set vibrating by giving each point a velocity v = kx(L-x), determine the displacement u(x, t) at any time t.
options.topic: Solution of wave and heat conduction equation up to two dimension

options.label: b
options.text: Solve the Laplace equation d^2u/dx^2 + d^2u/dy^2 = 0 in a rectangle 0 < x < a, 0 < y < b, given that u(0, y) = 0, u(a, y) = 0, u(x, b) = 0 and u(x, 0) = f(x).

options.label: a
options.text: Calculate the first four moments about the mean for the following frequency distribution: (x: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) and (f: 1, 3, 7, 3, 1). Also compute the value of Kurtosis.

options.label: b
options.text: Find the moment generating function of a random variable X having the density function f(x) = e^(-x) for x > 0 and 0 otherwise. Use it to find the mean and variance.

options.label: a
options.text: If X is a normally distributed random variable with mean 50 and standard deviation 10, find P(X < 45) and P(30 < X < 60). (Given Area under standard normal curve from 0 to 0.5 is 0.1915, from 0 to 1 is 0.3413, and from 0 to 2 is 0.4772)

options.label: b
options.text: A discrete random variable X has the following probability distribution: (X: 0, 1, 2, 3) with probabilities (P: k, 2k, 2k, k^2). Find the value of k and calculate the expectation E(2X + 3).

options.label: a
options.text: In a sample of 1000 people, 540 are rice eaters and the rest are wheat eaters. Can we assume that both rice and wheat are equally popular in the population at 1% level of significance? (Critical value at 1% is 2.58)

options.label: b
options.text: The following data shows the number of defectives in 10 samples of size 100 each: 12, 15, 13, 18, 10, 14, 16, 11, 19, 12. Construct a p-chart and determine if the process is under control.
options.topic: Control Charts for Variables ( p, np and C charts)
